hero.title: Welcome to my future
hero.subtitle: Today, I'm building my future.
hero.intro1: I'm Herman Vanel, and I don't just imagine the future — I code it.
hero.intro2: I experiment, prototype, automate. I build systems that solve real problems.
hero.intro3: This site isn't a traditional resume. It's a living demonstration.

currentStatus.title: Where I am today
currentStatus.subtitle: A clear vision of my current situation

academic.title: Business Student
academic.description: Currently in my 3rd year of Business Techniques degree, in a work-study program. This training gives me a structured business vision, market understanding, and strategic skills.

builder.title: System Builder
builder.description: In parallel, I build. Trading for 3 years to understand systems and rigor. Automation and bots to create tools that work autonomously.

whyThisSite.title: Why this site replaces a traditional resume

living.title: Living
living.description: A paper resume is static. This site evolves, enriches, demonstrates my ability to create.

testable.title: Testable
testable.description: Don't believe me about automations? Test them directly on this site.

vision.title: Vision
vision.description: I'm not looking for a job, I'm building my future. This site is proof.

summary.part3Suffix: , they prepare me to create systems that matter.

projects.title: What I build
projects.subtitle: Concrete projects that demonstrate my vision

items.title: Algorithmic Trading
items.subtitle: The gateway to systems
items.description: Trading isn't an end in itself, it's a trigger. That's where I discovered the rigor of systems, the logic of algorithms, and the power of automation.

items.title: Intelligent Automations
items.subtitle: Workflows that work for me
items.description: I use n8n to create complex automations: AI image generation, data processing, multi-platform integrations. Systems that run 24/7.

items.title: Bots & Autonomous Assistants
items.subtitle: Agents that act
items.description: Bots that respond, analyze, decide. Autonomous systems that understand context and execute tasks without human intervention.

skills.title: What I learned by building
skills.subtitle: My skills don't just come from courses — they come from real projects, corrected mistakes, and varied experiences.

items.title: System Logic
items.description: Understanding how pieces fit together. Thinking in flows, processes, architecture.

items.title: Automation
items.description: n8n, webhooks, APIs. Creating systems that run without constant human supervision.

items.title: Artificial Intelligence
items.description: Integrating AI into concrete workflows. GPT, image generation, data analysis.

items.title: Discipline & Rigor
items.description: Trading taught me patience, methodical analysis, and risk management.

items.title: Autonomy
items.description: Learning by myself. Searching, testing, failing, iterating, succeeding.

items.title: Teaching
items.description: Break dance teacher and coach for years. I learned to teach, motivate, and guide.

origin.title: Where do these skills come from?
origin.paragraph1: These skills don't come from nowhere. They are the result of a journey combining academic training (Business degree in work-study), field experiences (animation, hip-hop, professional immersions), and personal builds (trading, automation, bots).
origin.paragraph2: I learned that pedagogy and leadership acquired through teaching are just as important as technical mastery. One without the other is incomplete.
origin.paragraph3: → See my detailed journey in the timeline section above

timeline.title: My Journey
timeline.subtitle: A coherent path between academic training, field experiences, and personal builds

events.title: Animator & Hip-hop Assistant Teacher
events.description: Youth supervision, technique transmission, group management. I developed skills in pedagogy, responsibility, and leadership.

events.title: Discovering the professional world
events.description: Industry internships and volunteer missions to understand how organizations work, team dynamics, and operational processes.

events.title: Business Techniques Degree (3rd year)
events.description: Work-study program that brings me a structured business vision, market understanding, and skills in commercial strategy and marketing.

events.title: Trading & Automation
events.description: Algorithmic trading for 3 years: gateway to rigor, system logic, and automation. Bot and automated workflow development in parallel.

events.title: AI & Development Master's
events.description: Planned continuation of studies in a master's program focused on artificial intelligence and development (Epitech or equivalent) to deepen my technical skills.

timeline.coherence: Each step of this journey brought me complementary skills. Academic training structures my business vision, while my personal builds develop my technical mastery.
timeline.academicBold: Academic training structures my business vision
timeline.personalBold: my personal builds develop my technical mastery

vision.title: What I want to build tomorrow
vision.intro1: I don't build for the sake of building.
vision.intro2: Free to choose my projects. Free to work on what matters. Free to experiment without limits.
vision.intro3: Technology isn't an end — it's a
vision.intro3End: . A means to create systems that work, that last, that serve.
goals: Create automation systems that simplify daily life
Leverage AI to solve concrete problems
Stay curious, learn continuously, experiment endlessly
vision.conclusion1: I'm not looking for a job.

technical.title: 💡 Technical Architecture
technical.description: This feature is ready to be connected to an n8n workflow. It demonstrates my ability to integrate APIs, manage webhooks, and create reactive user interfaces. The code is structured to be easily extensible and maintainable.
